title: Chocolate Olive Oil Cake
description: My Thermomix reimagining of Nigella's chocolate olive oil cake — dark, damp and dairy-free.
intro: Nigella's olive oil cake is gloriously dark and grown-up, and there's no butter in sight. The Thermomix makes the batter in one bowl in minutes — then your oven does the rest.
ingredients: 150 g olive oil, plus extra for the tin
steps: Place the cocoa and boiling water in the mixing bowl with the vanilla. Mix 10 sec / 0°C / speed 4 to a smooth paste, then tip into a small bowl and set aside.
Without washing the bowl, add the sugar, olive oil and eggs. Mix 30 sec / 0°C / speed 4.
Add the almond meal, bicarbonate of soda, salt and the cocoa paste. Mix 20 sec / 0°C / speed 4, scrape down, then 10 sec / 0°C / speed 3.

Pour into a greased and lined 22 cm tin. Bake in a preheated oven at 170°C for 38–40 minutes, until the top is set and a skewer comes out with a few moist crumbs. (The Thermomix makes the batter; baking is done in your oven.); finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
Cool in the tin for 10 minutes before turning out; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: Almond meal keeps it damp and gluten-free; swap for 125 g plain flour if you prefer.
Don't overbake — a few moist crumbs on the skewer is perfect.
Beautiful with a spoon of coconut yoghurt and berries.

faq.a: Yes — it uses olive oil instead of butter, so it's naturally dairy-free. Serve with coconut yoghurt to keep it that way.
It already is if you use almond meal. Just check your cocoa and baking ingredients are gluten-free too.

faq.q: Is this the original Nigella Lawson recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Nigella Lawson and chocolate olive oil cake; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
